target.name: StoreDot
target.founder: Doron Myersdorf, Simon Litsyn, Gil Rosenman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $206M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
StoreDot, a Series E climate and energy company, has raised $206.5M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector comprises 9 comparable firms (1 public: Lonza Group; 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems), though direct peer valuation data at StoreDot's stage remains sparse.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, uses the $206M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement to subsequent funding rounds, yielding $289M. This represents the median point of the analysis. A conservative range incorporates sensitivity around this multiplier: the low bound of $246M reflects a more modest 1.2x step-up, while the $332M high bound reflects a 1.6x progression factor.

The single-method reliance reflects a material constraint: while 19 same-sector peers exist, insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with published valuations preclude robust peer-median triangulation. The implied valuation range of $246M–$332M (median $289M) is therefore contingent on the applicability of stage-based step-up dynamics to StoreDot's fundraising trajectory. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peer cohort, market timing of next funding event, and whether public-market climate-tech valuations (proxied by Lonza) remain informative for private frontier-tech risk profiles.
